primaryStudies.citation: Bornstein NM, Guekht A, Vester J, et al. Safety and efficacy of Cerebrolysin in early post-stroke recovery: a meta-analysis of nine randomized clinical trials. Neurol Sci. 2018;39(4):629-640.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Cerebrolysin significantly improved early motor function recovery (ARAT) and global neurological status (NIHSS); favorable safety profile comparable to placebo

primaryStudies.citation: Guekht A, Vester J, Heiss WD, et al. Safety and efficacy of Cerebrolysin in motor function recovery after stroke: a meta-analysis of the CARS trials. Neurol Sci. 2017;38(10):1761-1769.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Superior motor recovery with Cerebrolysin vs placebo at day 90; effect sustained in NIHSS and Barthel Index subscales

primaryStudies.citation: Muresanu DF, Heiss WD, Hoemberg V, et al. Cerebrolysin and Recovery After Stroke (CARS): A Randomized, Placebo-Controlled, Double-Blind, Multicenter Trial. Stroke. 2016;47(1):151-159.
primaryStudies.population: 208 patients with acute ischemic stroke (middle cerebral artery territory)
primaryStudies.keyFindings: CARS 1: Cerebrolysin 30mL/day for 21 days significantly improved Action Research Arm Test vs placebo at day 90; NNT=5 for clinically meaningful improvement

primaryStudies.citation: Heiss WD, Brainin M, Bornstein NM, et al. Cerebrolysin in patients with acute ischemic stroke in Asia: results of a double-blind, placebo-controlled randomized trial. Stroke. 2012;43(3):630-636.
primaryStudies.population: 1070 patients with acute ischemic stroke (Asia: China, South Korea, Iran)
primaryStudies.keyFindings: CASTA trial: No significant difference in primary endpoint (ADOS) at day 90, but significant benefit in severe stroke subgroup and early improvement

primaryStudies.citation: Ladurner G, Kalvach P, Moessler H; Cerebrolysin Study Group. Neuroprotective treatment with cerebrolysin in patients with acute stroke: a randomised controlled trial. J Neural Transm. 2005;112(3):415-428.
primaryStudies.population: 146 patients with acute middle cerebral artery stroke
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Cerebrolysin 50mL for 21 days improved clinical global impression and neurological outcomes vs placebo; favorable safety

primaryStudies.citation: Alvarez XA, Cacabelos R, Laredo M, et al. A 24-week, double-blind, placebo-controlled study of three dosages of Cerebrolysin in patients with mild to moderate Alzheimer's disease. Eur J Neurol. 2006;13(1):43-54.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: 10mL and 30mL doses improved ADAS-cog and CIBIC+ scores vs placebo at 24 weeks; effect persisted 3 months post-treatment

primaryStudies.citation: Ruether E, Husmann R, Kinzler E, et al. A 28-week, double-blind, placebo-controlled study with Cerebrolysin in patients with mild to moderate Alzheimer's disease. Int Clin Psychopharmacol. 2001;16(5):253-263.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Significant improvement in cognitive function (ADAS-cog) and global clinical impression vs placebo; benefits sustained at 3-month follow-up

primaryStudies.citation: Panisset M, Gauthier S, Moessler H, et al. Cerebrolysin in Alzheimer's disease: a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial with a neurotrophic agent. J Neural Transm. 2002;109(7-8):1089-1104.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Cerebrolysin 30mL 5x/week for 4 weeks improved ADAS-cog and CGI; therapeutic effect persisted 3 months after treatment cessation

primaryStudies.citation: Gauthier S, Proano JV, Jia J, et al. Cerebrolysin in mild-to-moderate Alzheimer's disease: a meta-analysis of randomized controlled clinical trials. Dement Geriatr Cogn Disord. 2015;39(5-6):332-347.
primaryStudies.population: 6 RCTs, 784 patients with mild-moderate Alzheimer's disease
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Significant improvement in global clinical impression (OR 3.32) and cognitive function; effect size comparable to cholinesterase inhibitors

primaryStudies.citation: Plosker GL, Gauthier S. Cerebrolysin: a review of its use in dementia. Drugs Aging. 2009;26(11):893-915.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Comprehensive efficacy and safety review; evidence supports use in Alzheimer's disease and vascular dementia; well-tolerated

primaryStudies.citation: Chen N, Yang M, Guo J, et al. Cerebrolysin for vascular dementia. Cochrane Database Syst Rev. 2013;(1):CD008900.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Evidence suggests benefit on global clinical impression; limited evidence quality; no significant adverse effects vs placebo

primaryStudies.citation: Ziganshina LE, Abakumova T, Vernay L. Cerebrolysin for acute ischaemic stroke. Cochrane Database Syst Rev. 2017;4(4):CD007026.
primaryStudies.population: 6 RCTs, 1501 patients with acute ischemic stroke
primaryStudies.keyFindings: No convincing evidence of benefit on mortality or dependence; trend toward early neurological improvement; more evidence needed

primaryStudies.citation: Amiri-Nikpour MR, Nazarbaghi S, Hamdi-Holasou M, Rezaei Y. An open-label evaluator-blinded clinical study of cerebrolysin plus rivastigmine versus rivastigmine alone in patients with mild to moderate Alzheimer's disease. Clin Neuropharmacol. 2017;40(5):199-204.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Combination of Cerebrolysin + rivastigmine superior to rivastigmine alone on MMSE and ADAS-cog at 28 weeks

primaryStudies.citation: Zhang C, Chopp M, Cui Y, et al. Cerebrolysin enhances neurogenesis in the ischemic brain and improves functional outcome after stroke. J Neurosci Res. 2010;88(15):3275-3281.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Cerebrolysin enhanced neurogenesis, angiogenesis, and functional recovery; effects mediated via sonic hedgehog signaling pathway

primaryStudies.citation: Rockenstein E, Mante M, Adame A, et al. Effects of Cerebrolysin on neurogenesis in an APP transgenic model of Alzheimer's disease. Acta Neuropathol. 2007;113(3):265-275.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Cerebrolysin increased neurogenesis, reduced amyloid load, and improved behavioral deficits; neuroprotective mechanism confirmed

primaryStudies.citation: Hartbauer M, Hutter-Paier B, Windisch M. Effects of Cerebrolysin on the outgrowth and protection of processes of cultured brain neurons. J Neural Transm. 2001;108(5):581-592.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Cerebrolysin promotes neurite outgrowth and protects neurons from excitotoxicity; mimics neurotrophic factor activity

primaryStudies.citation: Masliah E, Diez-Tejedor E. The pharmacology of neurotrophic treatment with Cerebrolysin: brain protection and repair to counteract pathologies of acute and chronic neurological disorders. Drugs Today. 2012;48 Suppl A:3-24.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of neurotrophic mechanisms; multimodal neuroprotection via BDNF-like, GDNF-like, and CNTF-like activities

primaryStudies.citation: Ubhi K, Rockenstein E, Doppler E, et al. Neurofibrillary and neurodegenerative pathology in APP-transgenic mice injected with AAV2-mutant TAU: neuroprotective effects of Cerebrolysin. Acta Neuropathol. 2009;117(6):699-712.
primaryStudies.population: APP transgenic mice with tau pathology
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Cerebrolysin reduced tau phosphorylation, neurofibrillary pathology, and synaptic loss in AD mouse model

primaryStudies.citation: Muresanu DF, Buzoianu A, Florian SI, von Wild T. Towards a roadmap in brain protection and recovery. J Cell Mol Med. 2012;16(12):2861-2871.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Position paper on neurotrophic treatment; Cerebrolysin as multimodal neuroprotective agent activating endogenous defense mechanisms

primaryStudies.citation: Guekht AB, Moessler H, Novak PH, Gusev EI. Cerebrolysin in vascular dementia: improvement of clinical outcome in a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled multicenter trial. J Stroke Cerebrovasc Dis. 2011;20(4):310-318.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Cerebrolysin 20mL for 4 weeks improved ADAS-cog+ and CGI vs placebo; well-tolerated; significant effect on executive function

primaryStudies.citation: Alvarez XA, Fuentes P. Cerebrolysin in Alzheimer's disease. Drugs Today (Barc). 2011;47(7):487-513.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of Cerebrolysin in Alzheimer's; evidence from 10 RCTs supports efficacy on cognition and global function

primaryStudies.citation: Zhang L, Chopp M, Meier DH, et al. Sonic hedgehog signaling pathway mediates cerebrolysin-improved neurological function after stroke. Stroke. 2013;44(7):1965-1972.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Cerebrolysin activates sonic hedgehog pathway promoting neurorestorative effects; enhanced oligodendrogenesis and myelination

primaryStudies.citation: Xiao S, Yan H, Yao P; Cerebrolysin Study Group. Efficacy and safety of Cerebrolysin in patients with Alzheimer's disease in China: a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial. Alzheimers Dement. 2006;2(3):S361.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Chinese Phase 3 trial supporting efficacy in Alzheimer's disease; contributed to regulatory approval in China

primaryStudies.citation: Skvortsova VI, Stakhovskaia LV, Gubskii LV, et al. A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study of Cerebrolysin safety and efficacy in the treatment of acute ischemic stroke. Zh Nevrol Psikhiatr Im S S Korsakova. 2004;104(11):51-55.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Russian RCT: Cerebrolysin improved neurological outcomes; early initiation associated with better recovery

primaryStudies.citation: Hong Z, Moessler H, Bornstein N, et al. A double-blind, placebo-controlled, randomized trial to evaluate the safety and efficacy of Cerebrolysin in patients with acute ischaemic stroke in Asia--CASTA. Int J Stroke. 2009;4(5):406-412.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Largest Cerebrolysin stroke trial in Asia; protocol for multicenter trial in China, South Korea, and Iran

primaryStudies.citation: Stan AD, Ghiciuc CM, Mitu F, et al. Cerebrolysin combined with rehabilitation reduces cognitive impairment after stroke. J Neurol Sci. 2017;381:108.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Cerebrolysin combined with rehabilitation improves post-stroke cognitive outcomes

primaryStudies.citation: Muresanu DF, Alvarez XA, Moessler H, et al. Persistence of the effects of Cerebrolysin on cognition and qEEG slowing in vascular dementia patients: results of a 3-month extension study. J Neurol Sci. 2010;299(1-2):179-183.
primaryStudies.population: Vascular dementia patients from previous RCT
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Cognitive benefits persisted 3 months after treatment cessation; qEEG improvements correlated with clinical outcomes

primaryStudies.citation: Wei ZH, He QB, Wang H, Su BH, Chen HZ. Meta-analysis: the efficacy of nootropic agent Cerebrolysin in the treatment of Alzheimer's disease. J Neural Transm. 2007;114(5):629-634.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Early Chinese meta-analysis confirming efficacy in AD; significant improvement in CGI and cognitive measures

primaryStudies.citation: Chen CC, Wei ST, Tsaia SC, Chen XX, Cho DY. Cerebrolysin enhances cognitive recovery of mild traumatic brain injury patients: double-blind, placebo-controlled, randomized study. Br J Neurosurg. 2013;27(6):803-807.
primaryStudies.population: 32 patients with mild traumatic brain injury
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Cerebrolysin improved cognitive recovery (CogState) in mild TBI; faster return to baseline function

primaryStudies.citation: Onose G, Muresanu DF, Ciurea AV, et al. Neuroprotective and consequent neurorehabilitative clinical outcomes, in patients treated with the pleiotropic drug cerebrolysin. J Med Life. 2009;2(4):350-360.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Romanian clinical experience with Cerebrolysin in neurorehabilitation; pleiotropic neuroprotective effects review

primaryStudies.citation: Wong GKC, Zhu XL, Poon WS. Beneficial effect of cerebrolysin on moderate and severe head injury patients: result of a cohort study. Acta Neurochir Suppl. 2005;95:59-60.
primaryStudies.keyFindings: Cerebrolysin associated with improved outcomes in traumatic brain injury patients

russianStudies.citation: Gusev EI, Skvortsova VI. Cerebrolysin in acute ischemic stroke: experience of randomized clinical trials. Zh Nevrol Psikhiatr Im S S Korsakova. 2009;Suppl:26-38.
russianStudies.keyFindings: Comprehensive Russian review of Cerebrolysin evidence in stroke; widely used in Russian clinical practice

russianStudies.citation: Levin OS, Batukaeva LA, Smolentseva IG, Iunischenko NA. Efficacy of Cerebrolysin in vascular cognitive impairment. Zh Nevrol Psikhiatr. 2007;107(8):20-24.
russianStudies.keyFindings: Russian trial supporting efficacy in vascular cognitive impairment; routine use in Russian neurology practice

austrianStudies.citation: Windisch M, Gschanes A, Hutter-Paier B. Neurotrophic activities and therapeutic experience with a brain derived peptide preparation. J Neural Transm Suppl. 1998;53:289-298.
austrianStudies.keyFindings: EVER Pharma foundational research on neurotrophic mechanisms; peptide composition analysis and activity characterization

austrianStudies.citation: Windisch M. Approach towards an integrative drug treatment of Alzheimer's disease. J Neural Transm Suppl. 2000;59:301-313.
austrianStudies.keyFindings: Austrian research on integrative treatment approach with neurotrophic peptides in neurodegeneration

chineseStudies.citation: Xiao S, Xue HB, Li JS, et al. Therapeutic effects of cerebrolysin added to donepezil in patients with Alzheimer's disease: a randomized controlled trial. Chin J Clin Rehabil. 2005;9(44):126-128.
chineseStudies.keyFindings: Combination therapy superior to donepezil alone in Chinese AD patients

chineseStudies.citation: Li SY, Jia JP, Dai W, et al. Multicenter randomized double-blind controlled study of Cerebrolysin for vascular dementia. Chin J Geriatr. 2009;28(5):401-404.
chineseStudies.keyFindings: Chinese Phase 3 trial supporting efficacy; contributed to CFDA approval

trials.title: Cerebrolysin Acute Stroke Treatment in Asia (CASTA)
trials.notes: Largest stroke trial; primary endpoint not met but positive subgroup effects

manufacturer.company: EVER Neuro Pharma GmbH (formerly EBEWE Pharma)
manufacturer.notes: Austrian pharmaceutical company; global distribution; over 50 years of Cerebrolysin production

evidenceSummary.regulatoryStatus: Approved in 50+ countries (Austria, Russia, China, Germany, etc.); NOT FDA-approved; no MHRA/Health Canada/TGA approval
evidenceSummary.keyLimitation: Mixed results in large Phase 3 trials; Cochrane reviews note limited evidence quality; positive in subgroups and meta-analyses but definitive evidence lacking

metadata.internationalScope: Austria, Russia, China, Germany, Romania, Asia-Pacific, Latin America
